title: 2015 Trek Verve 3
description:
The frame and all accessories are a very dark gray. Trek calls the color "Cosmic Black." There are a few very small blue accents on the frame in a few places. Shifters are light gray colored, with two levers on each for up / down shifting, and 24 speeds. It also has a front suspension fork with pre-load adjustment. There is a Bontrager rear rack attached. This model is shown (without a rear rack) in the Trek archive at 

http://archive.trekbikes.com/us/en/2015/Trek/verve_3

title: 2023 Cervélo ASPERO – 5 GRX RX815 Di2
description:
• Premium grade gravel race carbon GRX Di2/disc brake bicycle that improves on original design
• Upgraded carbon fiber, and lay-up schedule for stiffer, faster design with aerodynamic improvements
• Use of D-shaped, tapered carbon fork steerer design, with included bar & stem for full wire/housing integration including dropper post
• Change allows for more aero headtube design and reduced drag from integrated routing
• Lower, more stable road-like feel from overall geometry, chainstay length, BB drop
• Accepts 700c x 43mm tires / 650b x 51mm tires; minimum 4mm side clearance
• Trail Mixer fork dropouts can be switched for +/- 5mm selectable fork offset; 62mm optimal Trail
• Forward position=decreased trail=faster handling | Rearward position=increased trail=slower handling
• Squoval.Max tube designs, enlarged and enhanced for rough surfaces; fork/downtube integration
• Large downtube BBright bottom bracket shell w/ oversized dropped chainstays
• Carbon lay-up, round seatpost, larger volume tires contribute to rider comfort
• 1x/2x compatible, with mechanical or electronic shifting systems; removable braze-on FD mount
• Frameset
o Frame: Aspero-5 Carbon, full internal routing, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/142mm TA dropout
o Fork: Tapered Áspero-5 Fork, Adjustable Trail; D-Shaped steerer, Flat Mount caliper interface, 12/100mm TA dropout
o Headset: FSA IS-2 1¼ x 1½"
o Thru-axles: Cervélo indexed/threaded: 12/100mm, 12/142mm
• Groupset: Shimano GRX 815 Di2 2x11 w/ hydraulic disc braking; internal eTube wiring, seatpost battery
o Dual Control levers: Di2 Shifting with mineral oil based hydraulic braking set-up
o Brake Calipers/Rotors: Shimano GRX RX815 Hydraulic Disc w/ SM-RT800 CL 160mm rotors
o Derailleurs: GRX 815 Di2 Front (braze-on), Rear (up to 34T max cog)
o Crankset/BB: GRX 810 48/31T / Cervélo BBright JY-BB 24 bottom bracket
o Cassette/Chain: HG800 11-34T / CN701
• Wheelset & Tires
o Cervélo Reserve with 32mm Carbon rims (IRW 24mm), TLR, DT350 HG11, CL disc
o Panaracer Gravel King SK TLR 700x38
• Cockpit & Accessories
o Handlebar: Carbon AB09 16° Flared Carbon, 31.8mm, integrated routing; Cervélo bar tape
o Stem: Cervélo ST32 Alloy, 31.8, (for Cervélo D-shaped fork steerer tube, integrated routing)
o Seatpost: Cervélo SP19 Carbon 27.2mm round (48-54cm Zero offset, 56-61cm, 25mm setback)
o Saddle: Prologo Dimension Nack
o Cervélo accessories: Stem Faceplate Computer/Acc mount, Rear Accessory Mount, and Smartpak 400A Top Tube Storage Bag, Downtube Protector
• Component specifics by frame size:
o 48cm Bar width: 38c-c, Stem length: 70mm, Crank length: 165mm
• Colors: Five Black
• Sizes: 48 cm
• Weight: 18.69 lbs (56cm)

title: 2020 Jamis Hudson Step-Thru
description:
Think of our Hudson as part Explorer and part Earth Cruiser. Capable but cool. Casual but on it. We blended the flowing curves and shaped tubes of seemingly disparate platforms like our carbon Dakota 29’ers and our aluminum DXT’s and came up with a smart frame design that rides comfortably, casually and efficiently while making a bold statement all at the same time.

Hudson Lite
When it comes to recreational riding on a so-called comfort bike, nothing gets more uncomfortable faster than having to pedal more weight than necessary. Which is why the Hudson Sport is built with lightweight 6061 aluminum tubing. We design comfort into the naturally stiffer ride of the aluminum tubes by specifying large volume tires and lengthening the chainstays and wheelbase for a stretch-limo sort of ride.

Easy Rider
Specially designed for flat-footed starts and stops. With your weight evenly distributed over the crank for comfortable and efficient pedaling on the saddle or off. The Hudson’s laid back seat tube and lowered bottom bracket height allows riders to position their saddles for efficient leg extension and still easily reach the ground while seated for balance when stopped. The longer chainstays and wheelbase in tandem with the lower center of gravity offers a very smooth, stable ride.

Step Right In
Our step-through version is exceptionally easy to mount thanks to the low BB DROP and super-low position of the S-bent top tube. No more swinging a leg over the rear wheel, or pulling a knee up to the chest to step over the top tube to mount a bike.

Get Fit
All frame sizes offer an extra-long head tube and hi- rise handlebars for a comfortable, upright ride position.

Where's The Other Brake Lever?
The SureStop braking system simplifies stopping by requiring only one brake lever to operate both front & rear brakes. It makes riding safer too by reducing the risk of front wheel lock-up. SureStop uses the momentum of the rear wheel to actively modulate the front brake, enabling smooth, controlled stopping. With only one hand!

Best In Class Comfort
Our super-plush saddle features Memory Foam padding for optimum support with maximum comfort. The slip-resistant suede-like top prevents sliding and the burnished side panels reduce leg friction while pedaling. Elastomer springs also help to soak up road vibrations. Offered in gender-specific sizes.

Pump Up The Volume
Our Hudson tires are ready to roll, coming in a tall 27.5” diameter with a bump-chewing 2.35” width. The big benefit of that footprint is larger air volume in the tire and tube, which results in lower air pressure for a more comfortable ride than tires with smaller volumes and higher air pressures.

Clean And Simple
The Hudson’s alloy crankset features an outer-ring chainguard, with another full-length chainguard running the length of the chain from seatstay to chainring. So socks, slacks, skirts and dresses are well-protected from the chain.

- 6061 TIG-welded aluminum frame
- Hi-tensile unicrown fork
- Alex DC25 alloy double-wall wheel set
- 7-Speed Shimano Altus components

title: 2002 LeMond Racing Cycles Nevada City
description: 2002 Lemond Nevada City road bike, Reynolds 853 Steel, Grey/blue color (mainly gray), 58/59cm (sticker still on frame), 8spd Sora components, triple chain ring with center derailleur spring broken so I locked it into the middle chain ring, Village Cycle Sport sticker at far bottom base of seat tube, 32 spoke Aurora Matrix wheels with IRC tires (700x25mm and very worn looking, rear has some green paint specs on sidewall), very long 60mm “gold” front tire valve (rear valve dull gray short 35mm and leaks as soon as it is opened), comfort saddle, large rear tail light, no light on front only silver/white reflector, cycle computer mount but no computer (very sharp edges), two silver bottle cages, old style aluminum LOOK pedals (right side pedal is black with red float adjuster (set to zero), left side one blue no float adjuster), seat tube has a small piece of black electric tape to remember seat height for  a 2nd rider. Rear brake is open because the rear wheel is badly out of true. “Greg Lemond” signature on top tube. Handle bar stem angled up (not horizontal) with silver end cap, black handle bar tape was messed-up on the left side. Front wheel with silver wheel magnet on outward spoke and black speed sensor on right side of fork blade.

Customised with SLX cranks (black) and Customised with Boardman Performance Flat Pedals 17 (black and well beaten up)
Customised with a BX Ascend branded dropper post (internally routed and with lever on left hand side)
Customised with back VELO branded seat with air hole and small rectangular orange label on rear left side.
Frame number  A0A01AG2313 (the 3rd diget is probably A but may be N or H)
Rear tire is Continental Mountain King.  Front tire a Continental X King. Rear tire is newer.
Customised with black homemade light attachment glued under the seat (may have ben removed but will be glue residue under seat)
Paintwork damaged by cable rub above right fork – clear plastic plastic protection tape has been applied there.
Top tube has 3 slight wear marks from straps suspended bag.
Front plastic mudguard secured by cable ties (left hand side cable tie secured to the fork arch is black, uncut and removable).
Had three distinctive plastic tubes secured to handle bars by cable ties (may now have been removed)
Right hand handle shows clear scrape/impact damage on its end
Right break lever bites early
General condition is OK though will show clear sign of use with various scratches and scrapes etc

title: Drossiger CXA2.1 cyclocross
description:
Men’s black and white cyclocross bike - road style bike with drop handlebars (scuffed on the right side) and 35mm tyres with tread. Drossiger frame with single bottle holder, cleated pedals, silver crankset, woman’s saddle and Race Face handlebar stem (stem, saddle and crankset replaced so differ from the sales image). At least one of the plastic caps on the hoods of the handlebars is missing. When it was stolen, it had a black saddlebag of tools and pump clipped to it as well as a red and white tag below the seat post for Sanctuary Students University Accomodation in Dundee. 
I’ve had this bike since I was fourteen. It has significant sentimental value and is possibly the worst material thing someone could have stolen from me. It’s taken me from John O’ Groats to Land’s End, from the top of Shetland to the Mull of Galloway and the length of the Irish West Coast and we weren’t done yet.

title: GT Bicycles Transeo 4.0
description:
The GT Transeo 4.0 is a hybrid bike designed for riders who want something versatile — comfortable for commuting and fitness rides, but also capable of handling light trails and gravel. Here are the main details about it:
Overview
Category: Hybrid / Fitness / Commuter
Brand: GT Bicycles 
Frame: Aluminum (lightweight, durable, upright geometry)
Fork: Equipped with a front suspension fork with a lockout option for efficient road riding
Wheels: 700c wheels (like a road bike, but wider tires for stability and comfort)
Speeds/Drivetrain: Typically a 3x8 (24-speed) Shimano or microSHIFT drivetrain
Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes (reliable stopping power in all weather, but not hydraulic)
Tires: 700x40c (good balance of speed and shock absorption)
Other features: Rack and fender mounts, blue MEC carrying panniers.

title: Supercycle BREEZE
description:
Color: Dark blue frame with a glossy finish. The paint shows minor scuffs and wear, particularly near the bottom bracket and chainstay, reflecting typical usage.

Frame Size & Design: The bike features a medium-sized frame with a slightly slanted top tube. The tube joints are visibly welded and reinforced, providing extra durability for off-road or urban riding.

Gears & Drivetrain: Equipped with a standard multi-speed gear setup. It has a triple chainring in the front and a 7-speed cassette in the rear, allowing for smooth shifting over a range of terrains. The derailleurs and shifters are operational but show minor signs of use.

Brakes: The bike uses traditional rim brakes, both front and rear, which are in good working order. The brake levers on the handlebars are black and slightly worn from use.

Handlebars: The handlebars are flat, straight, and black, with wear visible on the grips. The right grip end has a small cap missing, exposing the inner handlebar tube. The handlebar stem shows slight rust and wear, while the cables for the brakes and shifters are externally routed, curving smoothly toward the frame.

Tires & Wheels: The tires are thick, knobby, and designed for multi-surface riding, with a deep tread pattern ideal for gravel or rough urban surfaces. The tires are mounted on aluminum rims, which appear to be in good shape. Both wheels are secured with standard quick-release mechanisms.

Saddle: The saddle is a basic black vinyl model, showing slight wear but no visible damage. It's supported by a standard seat post with a quick-release mechanism for easy height adjustments.

Water Bottle & Holder: White water bottle that says 'Silvera for Seniors' is a sipper style 500 ml bottle and is on a metal bottle cage that is located in the vertical rod of the bike just above the front gear chain

Accessories:

Lock: A black coiled cable lock is attached around the frame, securing the bike when parked.
Green Bag: A small green bag is mounted on the handlebar stem, possibly used for small items or tools.
Reflectors: There are red rear reflectors and front-mounted white reflectors for visibility.
Bar Code: The bike features a barcode sticker on the frame near the handlebar with the number "70410 01847."

Distinctive Marks: The handlebar stem has visible rust and slight wear, while the grips show signs of extended use.

title: 1994 Nishiki Optima XRS
description:
The bike was taken from 1512 South Columbus Street, Arlington, VA 22204 (a residential property near Wakefield High School/Four Mile Run).  It was taken the night of Friday, March 15, 2019/morning of Saturday, March 16, 2019. 

The serial number (R907640323) may be difficult to read (3s may actually be 8s).  The Optimas were only made in 1993 and 1994.  I believe mine is a 1994 based on the serial number, but it may be a 1993.  The frame is steel and chromoly.  The seat tube length is 22" (possibly 20" or 21.5").  The bike is a light, mint green.  

When it was taken, it had a black rack on the rear wheel, silver fenders, two silver water bottle holders, silver pedals with a flat surface on one side and shoe clip attachments on the other (Shimano PD-M324), U-lock mount on the seat tube, two light mounts on the seat tube, grey handlebar grips, silver kickstand, black/silver bell, 700x28mm tires with silver rims (Alexrims), purple stuffed animal hidden under seat, black seat, black battery pack on front wheel hub to power LED lights on wheel spokes.  

Note: When the bike was taken, it did not have the handlebar extensions on it seen in pictures.

Troll mountain bike, with goofy gold+orange+black paint job, rear rack and two repairs on the frame. Stolen with a few other cycles from my garage on the south side of Bernal, San Francisco on the night of Wed Jan 8, 2020.

The top of the seat tube was recently repaired and is painted solid black. The rear rack eyelet at the top of the right seat stay has a repair which is highlighted in an old photo. The handlebar had little stubby GS3-L bar-ends. It has an aluminum bashguard carefully bolted to the big ring of a triple chain ring. It was a bit of a beast and I really liked it.

title: 2019 EEB Frame/Vector/Stealth Bomber B52, Ebike
description:
Hey all, just got my $3500 ebike stolen by a guy who said he wanted to buy it. He said he wouldn't buy it unless he tried it. God I hate myself right now. If anyone sees it posted to sell, let me know. Here's some pictures of the bike and his fake ID. 

He is a late 30's early 40's 6'3-6'5" caucasian male with light blue/green eyes. His hair is long and slicked back and has facial hair identical to the photo provided. No visible tattoos. He wore a long sleeve green jacket with tan pants.

Prior to the sheriffs arrival to my location, they were responding to a call to a nearby street for 2 men loading a bike into a u-haul attached to a red Cadillac and the callers said the whole situation seemed very suspicious. Unfortunately they did not make it in time. The sheriffs think that was my bike or the thieves were stealing multiple bikes in the area.

We met up at Yes Plaza in Rowland Heights because the plaza is well lit and the majority of the restaurants were still open during that time. The location was also in front of a Los Angeles County Sheriff service station (it turns out its only operated by volunteers and open during specific hours).

He approached me from a direction I wasn't looking at and said his girlfriend dropped him off and that she was getting gas for the truck. Initially I refused his request to demo my bike and I went around the parking lot a few times to show him that the bike is functioning. I should've stuck with my gut or at least held all his possessions as a deposit. In hindsight the thief seemed pushy and I believe he was trying to distract me from looking too closely at his ID.

I should've known better but one thing that really threw me off is when he said "I can't buy this unless I try it" I thought it was a fair point as it's a pretty expensive 2nd hand purchase. Theft wasn't on my mind as much as liability. What if he hit someone or a car? Or dropped the bike? Taking off with the bike was on the lower end of the list of my worries at the time.

There was no way for me to catch the thief as the bike reaches up to 50 mph on flat ground when the speed limiter is off.

I don't expect I'll be getting my bike back but I hope this guy gets what he deserves. 


The following are defining specs of the electric bike
EBIKE1 in reflective tape on the side of the frame
Fox 40 forks
Black 100amp controller
72v 30ah battery 
80/100 - 21 Motorcycle tubes
26" Schwalbe big apple road tires
Enduro stealth bomber frame /vector frame 
6 speed Shimano cassette w/ derailleur 
Blue under glow lightning
Scratch on right side due to loose BB that follows the curve of the pedal path

Reward for info leading to arrest or return of the bike

title: 2018 Rocky Mountain Bicycles Altitude C90
description:
Pretty much out of the box Altitude C90 model, with Shimano XT all-mountain SPD/platform pedals (M785 Trail). Pedal arms have booties at the ends to protect them from smashing the ground, Rocky-provided frame protector was never installed (instead, the bike has a long length of clear plastic adhesive on the downtube), there's a small scratch/knick on the drive side of the seat tube near the bottom bracket from when I had a spare tube zip-tied to the frame, after-market Chromag Squarewave grips. Also has a black Marsh Guard front fender with teal lettering.
